description: single eq on uint256, no issues
unassigned opcode 0x30 (outside value and length ranges)
unassigned opcode at the upper edge of the gap is rejected
notBetween(5,10) is a satisfiable disjunction, no issues
negated lengthBetween(5,10) is a satisfiable disjunction, no issues
byte-identical eq(5) three times reports one duplicate
dominated bound in group 0, eq/neq in group 1
9 neq holes then eq of the 9th: cap drops it, no contradiction
33-step path exceeds the reference enforcer depth cap of 32
quantifier over uint256[300] exceeds the 256 iteration cap
every defined context property is recognized and typed: eq on address props, gte on uint256 props
neq under any() is satisfied by a decoy element
neq under all() is the correct blocklist encoding
eq on bytes4 with left-aligned operand, no issues
eq on bytes4 with right-aligned operand can never match
isIn on bytes4 with one right-aligned member
operator byte in the unassigned opcode gap is rejected
eq with a two-word payload is rejected
gte(10) and lte(100) fuse into between
lengthGte(2) and lengthLte(8) fuse into lengthBetween
negated gte with lte does not fuse
